description:
You are acting as an assistant to a data-scientist who keeps a very small,
    single-user project under the directory /home/user/ds_cleaning.

    Files that already exist before you start:
      1. /home/user/ds_cleaning/VERSION  
         • Contains one line only: 1.0.0 (followed by a single UNIX newline).

      2. /home/user/ds_cleaning/CHANGELOG.md  
         • The whole file is shown below:  
           ------------------------------------------------------------
           # Changelog

           All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

           ## [1.0.0] - 2022-12-01
           ### Added
           - Initial release
           ------------------------------------------------------------

    What the user wants

    1. Bump the project to the next PATCH version (= 1.0.1) using Semantic Versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).  
       • No other component (MAJOR, MINOR) should change.  
       • The VERSION file must end with a single trailing newline and contain *only* the new version string.

    2. Prepend a new entry to /home/user/ds_cleaning/CHANGELOG.md following **exactly** the template below  
       (do not remove the existing 1.0.0 section; just insert the new one right above it):

           ## [1.0.1] - 2023-01-15
           ### Changed
           - Cleaned dataset column names to snake_case in cleaned_data.csv.

       Formatting rules that the automated checker will enforce:  
       • The four lines shown above must be inserted as-is (including spaces, capitalisation and the final period).  
       • There must be a single blank line *before* the “## [1.0.1] …” header and a single blank line *after* the list item.  
       • The file must still start with “# Changelog” as its first line and must end with a newline.

    3. Create a plain-text log file called /home/user/version_bump.log with **exactly** the two lines below
       (no extra spaces, no extra blank lines):

           OldVersion:1.0.0
           NewVersion:1.0.1

    Summary of artefacts whose existence & content will be tested after you finish:
      • /home/user/ds_cleaning/VERSION         (now holding “1.0.1\n” only)  
      • /home/user/ds_cleaning/CHANGELOG.md    (new section added exactly as specified)  
      • /home/user/version_bump.log            (two-line log as specified)

    A successful solution can be achieved with roughly 2-3 shell commands
    (e.g. echo, sed, printf, etc.) plus any directory creation needed for the log.
truth:
Initial state (before the agent runs):

    Directory: /home/user/ds_cleaning
      ├── VERSION
      │     └── Content: "1.0.0\n"
      └── CHANGELOG.md
            # Changelog
            (newline)
            All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
            (newline)
            (newline)
            ## [1.0.0] - 2022-12-01
            ### Added
            - Initial release
            (newline at EOF)

  Target / verification state (after the agent runs):

    1. /home/user/ds_cleaning/VERSION
         Content (byte-exact): "1.0.1\n"

    2. /home/user/ds_cleaning/CHANGELOG.md
         Complete content expected (␤ represents a UNIX newline):

           # Changelog␤
           ␤
           All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.␤
           ␤
           ## [1.0.1] - 2023-01-15␤
           ### Changed␤
           - Cleaned dataset column names to snake_case in cleaned_data.csv.␤
           ␤
           ## [1.0.0] - 2022-12-01␤
           ### Added␤
           - Initial release␤

    3. /home/user/version_bump.log
         Content (byte-exact, newline at EOF):

           OldVersion:1.0.0␤
           NewVersion:1.0.1␤
